rulings.Case Title: FIDELITY AND DEPOSIT CO. OF MARYLAND AS SUBROGEE VS. H.K. FIRE PROTECTION, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication As To 1 Operative Complaint Of Plaintiff Fidelity And Deposit Co Of Maryland As Subrogee To Wells Fargo & Co And 2 Operative Complaint In Intervention Of Intervener Amco Insurance Company
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar Tuesday, July 3, 2018, Line 2, DEFENDANT H.K. FIRE PROTECTION, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication As To 1 Operative Complaint Of Plaintiff Fidelity And Deposit Co Of Maryland As Subrogee To Wells Fargo & Co And 2 Operative Complaint In Intervention Of Intervener Amco Insurance Company. Defendant H.K. Fire Protection, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment and its alternative motion for summary adjudication as to the complaint filed by plaintiff Fidelity and Deposit Co. of Maryland (Zurich) and the complaint in intervention filed by intervenor Amco Insurance Company is: a) granted as to Zurich's cause of action for declaratory relief and b) denied as to the causes of action for negligence alleged by Zurich and Amco and the breach of contract cause of action alleged by Zurich. The declaratory relief cause of action is unnecessary and duplicative of Zurich's other claims and therefore summary adjudication is granted in favor of HK on Zurich's first cause of action per CCP 1061.There are triable disputes whether: a) the leak in the suppression system was in existence by the time HK completed its repair and inspection work on October 19, 2015; b) HK acted negligently in not finding the leak; c) the work undertaken by HK included providing information to the restaurant about the need to conduct monthly inspections; d) there is an implied contract requiring HK to provide information to the restaurant about the need to conduct monthly inspections; and e) HK's failure to find the leak and/or provide information to the restaurant about the need to conduct monthly inspections was a substantial factor in the harm allegedly suffered by the landlord and the restaurant. Liberally construed, the declarations and deposition testimony submitted by Zurich and Amco, particularly from their experts, HK's expert and Mr. Tang, establish a sufficient factual basis for the jury to decide in favor of Zurich and Amco on each of the foregoing issues. The facts submitted by HK tending to show that the restaurant acted negligently go to the issue of whether the restaurant was comparatively negligent and do not support granting summary adjudication in favor of HK on the negligence and contract claims. HK was sufficiently apprised of the issues on this motion by the complaints filed by Zurich and Amco. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Zurich is required to prepare a proposed order which complies with CCP 437c(g) and repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: CHRISTINA FERNANDEZ VS. SEPHORA USA, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff'S Motion To Compel Production Of Documents And Further Testimony Of Lauren Melinkoff
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar Tuesday, July 3, 2018, Line 6, Plaintiff'S Motion To Compel Production Of Documents And Further Testimony Of Lauren Melinkoff. Plaintiff Christina Fernandez's motion to compel production of documents and further testimony of Lauren Melinkoff is granted in part. No later than July 10, 2018 defendant Sephora USA, Inc. must provide a declaration under penalty of perjury from its custodian of records or other employee stating what efforts were made to locate all P4Ds for Ms. Fernandez and Ms. Melinkoff and the results of those efforts. If any P4Ds were located for Ms. Fernandez and Ms. Melinkoff, they need to be produced to Ms. Fernandez at the same time the declaration is provided to Ms. Fernandez. Plaintiff Christina Fernandez's motion to compel production of documents and further testimony of Lauren Melinkoff is Denied on the grounds that Sephora has shown that, after a reasonable diligent search and inquiry, the requested P4Ds are not in Sephora's custody, possession or control. =302/hk Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Ms. Fernandez is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: SAN ANTONIO SHOE INC A CORPORATION VS. T DAY, CORP A CORPORATION DBA NEW STEP #2, AKA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order To Compel Answers To Interrogatories And Responses To Demand For Inspection Of Documents And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, July 3, 2018, Line 2, PLAINTIFF SAN ANTONIO SHOE INC's Motion For Order To Compel Answers To Interrogatories And Responses To Demand For Inspection Of Documents And For Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge James Fleming, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Plaintiff's motion to compel answers to interrogatories and response to demand for inspection of documents and for sanctions is unopposed and will be granted. Defendant Barchard shall serve answers to interrogatories, response to demand for inspection of documents and responsive documents, all without objection, within 15 days of the date of order. Sanctions of $560.00 are awarded in favor of plaintiff and against defendant Barchard, payable within 15 days of the date of order, provided that the Court will revisit the amount of sanctions in the event counsel to plaintiff is required to attend an in-person hearing on this motion. Plaintiff's counsel shall submit a proposed order in Word format consistent with the foregoing by email to jameshfleming@gmail.com prior to the hearing. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to jameshfleming@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. Email submission of a proposed order as stated above will satisfy this requirement.=(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: AARON DRENBERG VS. JAMES K. SONG JR.
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar Tuesday, July 3, 2018, Line 4, DEFENDANT JAMES SONG, JR.'S MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD. B. Douglas Robbins' motion to withdraw as attorney of record for defendant James Song, Jr. is denied. Neither Mr. Robbins nor anyone else has ever been the attorney of record for Mr. Song in this case since Mr. Song has not appeared in this case. Neither the payment of a first appearance fee on behalf of Mr. Song nor the statement in papers filed by Catherine Song that the Wood Robbins firm and Mr. Robbins are attorneys for Mr. Song constitutes an appearance by Mr. Song. The court does not need to and does not address the issue of concern to plaintiff Aaron Drenberg whether service of papers on the Wood Robbins firm was valid service on Mr. Song. That issue is not germane to this motion. Both sides' requests for sanctions are denied. because neither side filed any papers or took any action that is fairly described as frivolous or solely intended for purposes of delay. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Mr. Robbins or someone else with his firm is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: TRAVELERS PROPERTY CASUALTY COMPANY OF AMERICA VS. HCI SYSTEM INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses Of Plaintiff To Form Interrogatories, Requests For Production Of Documents
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, July 3, 2018, Line 4, DEFENDANT HCI SYSTEMS, INC.'s Motion To Compel Further Responses Of Plaintiff To Form Interrogatories, Requests For Production Of Documents.(part 1 of 2) Pro Tem Judge James Fleming, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Defendant's motion to compel further responses to form interrogatories 12.2 and 12.3 will be granted to the extent hereinafter set forth. Defendant's motion to compel response to requests for production of documents will be denied as moot, the matter having resolved by agreement of the parties. The interrogatories in question seek, respectively, the identification of: 1. Persons interviewed concerning the incident giving rise to the action; 2. The interviewer(s); 3. Persons who gave a statement concerning the incident; 4. The recipient of the statement; 5. The date thereof; 6. The custodian of the statement. Plaintiff objected based attorney-client privilege and attorney work product protection. Plaintiff further asserted in its responses that there were no "interviews" or statements as requested by the interrogatories. In its moving papers, defendant has not expressly sought written or oral communications between plaintiff's attorneys of record and potential lay or expert witnesses or consultants. The Court finds that plaintiff has failed to carry its burden of establishing attorney-client privilege or attorney work product protection on a blanket basis. Indeed, plaintiff has failed to identify, except on a hypothetical basis, any attorney, client or work product and has not explained how any privilege could apply if there were no communications within the ambit of the interrogatories. (end of part 1 of 2) =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: TRAVELERS PROPERTY CASUALTY COMPANY OF AMERICA VS. HCI SYSTEM INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Hci Systems, Inc.'S Motion To Compel Further Responses Of Plaintiff To Form Interrogatories, Requests For Production Of Documents.(For Tentative Ruling Only)
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, July 3, 2018, Line 4, Defendant Hci Systems, Inc.'S Motion To Compel Further Responses Of Plaintiff To Form Interrogatories, Requests For Production Of Documents.(part 2 of 2)(For Tentative Ruling Only) In contradiction to its claim of no interviews or statements, plaintiff has acknowledged in its responses that communications occurred between plaintiff and its insured during the investigation and adjustment of the claim giving rise to this action. Plaintiff has not asserted the existence of an attorney-client relationship with its insured. Moreover, on plaintiff's own statement, the primary purpose of said communications was other than anticipation of litigation or for trial thus defeating any work product claim as to such communications. Further, plaintiff has argued that special rules of privilege analysis should apply to claims for subrogation but has failed to cite authority for that proposition. On the contrary, investigation and adjustment of casualty loss claims is part of the business of insurance, and not of the practice of law, even if a subrogation claim may ultimately be asserted. Plaintiff argues that there may be ambiguity or uncertainty in the term "incident" in a subrogation case. Plaintiff failed to object on that ground and any such objection has been waived. Lest there be any doubt, "incident" shall be understood in the ordinary sense of events leading to plaintiff's claim for subrogation. Likewise, the terms "interview" and "statement" as used in Interrogatories 12.2 and 12.3 shall be understood in the ordinary sense of oral and written communications. Accordingly, plaintiff shall serve further answers to form interrogatories 12.2 and 12.3 without objection, within 15 days of the date of order. This order shall have no application to communications between plaintiff's attorneys of record and potential lay or expert witnesses or consultants, but without prejudice to further discovery. The motion is otherwise denied. Sanctions are denied. Plaintiff's counsel shall submit a proposed order in Word format consistent with the foregoing by email to jameshfleming@gmail.com prior to the hearing Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to jameshfleming@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. Email submission of a proposed order as stated above will satisfy this requirement.=(302/JPT)
